Metadata.Coram: Woo Bih Li JAD, Debbie Ong Siew Ling JAD and See Kee Oon JAD
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: Appellate Division of the High Court of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Shree Ramkrishna Exports Pvt Ltd
Party_Details.Law_Firm: Oon & Bazul LLP / Audent Chambers LLC

Facts.Text: Shree Ramkrishna Exports Pvt Ltd is an India-incorporated company in the business of manufacturing and trading in jewellery and precious stones
SRK issued invoices to JGJ between 2016-09-02 and 2017-08-02 totalling US$23,400,456.25
A document titled 'Joint Venture Between JDM Group and The Jewelry Co. (SRK Group)' was sent by Amit to the Kriss Brothers
Vibhor informed Michael that SRK would not provide a comfort letter to defer payment by JGJ
The business collaboration between SRK and JGJ was officially terminated
JGJ's daily trial balances recorded the supply of Goods from SRK as purchases
JGJ's US counsel, Moses & Singer LLP, sent a letter to SRK's counsel rejecting SRK's demand for payment
JGJ was incorporated in Singapore as a vehicle for the parties' collaboration

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company is legally responsible to pay for goods supplied under invoices when the goods were allegedly supplied as capital contributions to a joint venture
Party_Details.Rule: A party claiming that goods were supplied as capital contributions must prove the terms of the joint venture agreement and that the goods were not intended to create a payment liability
Party_Details.Application: The invoices reflected legitimate sales and created a valid payment liability for the goods supplied.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the goods were supplied as sales and JGJ was liable to pay for them, but the amounts payable were adjusted based on reasonable compensation.

Metadata.Coram: Woo Bih Li JAD, Debbie Ong Siew Ling JAD and See Kee Oon JAD
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: Appellate Division of the High Court of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: J. G. Jewelry Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: J. G. Jewelry Pte Ltd is a Singapore-incorporated company in the business of trading jewellery and precious stones
JGJ was incorporated in Singapore as a vehicle for the parties' collaboration
A document titled 'Joint Venture Between JDM Group and The Jewelry Co. (SRK Group)' was sent by Amit to the Kriss Brothers
Vibhor informed Michael that SRK would not provide a comfort letter to defer payment by JGJ
The business collaboration between SRK and JGJ was officially terminated
JGJ's daily trial balances recorded the supply of Goods from SRK as purchases
JGJ's US counsel, Moses & Singer LLP, sent a letter to SRK's counsel rejecting SRK's demand for payment
A total sum of US$700,000 was injected into JGJ and described as 'Capital Introduction'

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company can rely on a joint venture agreement to avoid payment liability for goods supplied under invoices
Party_Details.Rule: A party seeking to avoid payment liability must demonstrate that the goods were supplied as capital contributions under a binding joint venture agreement
Party_Details.Application: The goods were supplied as capital contributions under a joint venture agreement and the invoices were not intended to create a payment liability.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the goods were supplied as sales and JGJ was liable to pay for them, but the amounts payable were adjusted based on reasonable compensation.

Metadata.Coram: Woo Bih Li JAD, Debbie Ong Siew Ling JAD and See Kee Oon JAD
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: Appellate Division of the High Court of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: J. G. Jewelry Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: J. G. Jewelry Pte Ltd is a Singapore-incorporated company in the business of trading jewellery and precious stones
Vibhor informed Michael that SRK would not provide a comfort letter to defer payment by JGJ
The business collaboration between SRK and JGJ was officially terminated
JGJ's daily trial balances recorded the supply of Goods from SRK as purchases
JGJ's US counsel, Moses & Singer LLP, sent a letter to SRK's counsel rejecting SRK's demand for payment
A total sum of US$700,000 was injected into JGJ and described as 'Capital Introduction'

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company can claim counter-restitution for payments made under invoices when the goods were allegedly supplied as capital contributions to a joint venture
Party_Details.Rule: A party claiming counter-restitution must prove that the payments were made under a mistake or that the goods were supplied as capital contributions under a binding joint venture agreement
Party_Details.Application: The payments were made under a mistake as the goods were supplied as capital contributions, entitling the company to counter-restitution.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the goods were supplied as sales and JGJ was liable to pay for them, but the amounts payable were adjusted based on reasonable compensation.

Metadata.Coram: Woo Bih Li JAD, Debbie Ong Siew Ling JAD and See Kee Oon JAD
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: Appellate Division of the High Court of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Shree Ramkrishna Exports Pvt Ltd
Party_Details.Law_Firm: Oon & Bazul LLP / Audent Chambers LLC

Facts.Text: Shree Ramkrishna Exports Pvt Ltd is an India-incorporated company in the business of manufacturing and trading in jewellery and precious stones
SRK issued invoices to JGJ between 2016-09-02 and 2017-08-02 for the Goods
A document titled 'Joint Venture Between JDM Group and The Jewelry Co. (SRK Group)' was sent by Amit to the Kriss Brothers
Vibhor informed Michael that SRK would not provide a comfort letter to defer payment by JGJ
The business collaboration between SRK and JGJ was officially terminated
JGJ's daily trial balances recorded the supply of Goods from SRK as purchases
On 2018-03-11, JGJ's US counsel rejected SRK's demand for payment

In May 2016, S Goldi, SRK's marketing arm, transferred funds to Shailesh, and Shailesh transferred a total of US$700,000 into JGJ. The remittance forms described the transfer as 'Capital Introduction'

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company is legally responsible to pay for goods supplied under invoices when the goods were allegedly supplied as capital contributions to a joint venture
Party_Details.Rule: A party claiming that goods were supplied as capital contributions must prove the terms of the joint venture agreement and that the goods were not intended to create a payment liability
Party_Details.Application: The invoices reflected legitimate sales, creating a valid payment liability, and the goods were not supplied as capital contributions.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the goods were supplied as sales and JGJ was liable to pay for them, but the amounts payable were adjusted based on reasonable compensation.
